FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Dsl

Classes

BoxEl

A box: container/layout node and/or a filled, optionally-clickable surface (VStack/HStack/Button/Box).

Element

Immutable description of a UI node (the "virtual DOM"). Cheap to build, never touches the scene directly.

Elevation

Fluent elevation presets — the engine's ANALYTIC drop shadows standing in for WinUI's ThemeShadow Z-depth model (we own every pixel; documented visual-equivalent to the DComp shadow). WinUI elevates popups by Z translation — base depth 32 + 8 per nested tier (ElevationHelper.cpp:19-21 s_elevationBaseDepth/s_elevationIterativeDepth) — and the design maps the popup bands to three depth CLASSES: ToolTip (lightest, depth-16 class) < Flyout/Menu (depth-32 class) < Dialog (depth-128 class). The preset blur/offset/alpha values keep the engine's existing visual scale; the CLASS a control uses is the contract: ToolTip → Tooltip; Flyout/MenuFlyout/CommandBarFlyout/TeachingTip → Flyout; ContentDialog → Dialog. Use via .Elevate(Elevation.Card) (Modifiers) or Ui.Card(...) which carries Card by default.

GridEl

A CSS-Grid container: column tracks (Pixel/Star/Auto) + gaps; children auto-flow row-major into cells. Rows take RowHeight (or the tallest cell when NaN). True tracks — every cell in a column shares its width (unlike nested-flex faking). Width-aware: resolves inside a ScrollView. (layout.md §7.)

IconGlyphs

Segoe Fluent glyph codepoints shared across LAYERS (a control and the engine recorder), so every consumer renders literally the same icon. Control-local glyphs stay at their call sites (or Controls.Icons); promote one here only when a layer below Controls needs it — today the scrollbar arrows, drawn both by the ScrollBar/AnnotatedScrollBar templates and by the engine's overlay scrollbar (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar).

ImageEl

An async image (album art): shows Placeholder until the decode lands, then the bitmap. Decode is off-thread + cached + residency-pinned while on screen (see ImageCache).

Modifiers

Fluent per-element style overrides (React/Compose/WinUI-style): Button("x", onClick).Background(c).Rounded(8). Each returns a record with-copy, so any default style is overrideable inline without forking the control.

Motion

The real Fluent 2 / WinUI motion durations + entrance metrics (durations only — the easing curves live in FluentGpu.Foundation.Easing, selected by the Hooks-layer motion helpers, since Dsl does not reference Animation). Canonical WinUI names (the motion audit's correction): ControlFaster = 83ms (BrushTransition, hover/press), ControlFast = 167ms (ControlFastAnimationDuration — knob travel, settle), ControlNormal = 250ms (ControlNormalAnimationDuration — reposition / pane motion).

PolylineStrokeEl

A text run. FontFamily selects the face — a system family name ("Segoe UI", "Segoe Fluent Icons") or a custom file as "path/to.ttf#Family Name" (the WinUI syntax). Null = the theme body font. Combine with an icon-font family + a private-use glyph to render icons (see Ui.Icon).

Radii

The WinUI Fluent corner-radius ramp (ControlCornerRadius / OverlayCornerRadius + the SelectorBar pill).

ScrollEl

A clipping, scrolling viewport over a single (possibly oversized) content child. Scroll is layout-free (layout.md §6): layout arranges the content at the content-box origin and publishes ContentSize; the -ScrollOffset translation is the content's LocalTransform, written by Input on wheel/drag.

Spacing

The WinUI Gallery spacing rhythm (page gutters, card spacing, internal padding, stack gaps).

SpanTextEl

A rich-text PARAGRAPH of typed inline runs (rtb-01 — the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines/RichTextBlock paragraph model: Run/Bold/Hyperlink): the spans concatenate and shape as ONE flow (one wrap pass — a styled word never re-flows independently of its sentence). Per-span weight/size/family/color/underline/strikethrough overlay the base style here; a span with OnClick is a HYPERLINK — the engine resolves the Hand cursor over its laid rects and fires the action on click (RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488 SetCursor(MouseCursorHand)); accent foreground + underline are the CALLER's styling (HyperlinkForeground, generic.xaml:1120-1122). No per-span italic: the shaper has no style axis yet (faces resolve by family+weight only).

TemplateParts

Lightweight template-part styling — the FluentGpu equivalent of CSS ::part / WinUI lightweight styling, signals-native. A control names its internal template parts (public const string PartHeader = "Header"; …) and routes each built part element through Apply<T>(TemplateParts?, string, T); app code layers ANY element props onto any part with a with expression:

Parts = new()
{
    [Expander.PartHeader] = b => b with
    {
        StickyTop = 8f,                                    // CSS position:sticky, top: 8px
        OnPinned  = p => stuck.Value = p,                  // the :stuck observable
        Fill = stuck.Value ? Tok.FillSolidBase : b.Fill,   // restyle ANYTHING off the signal
        BrushTransitionMs = Motion.ControlFast,            // …and the swap cross-fades
    },
}

Contract (apply order): control defaults → part modifier → the control RE-ASSERTS its mechanics-critical props (click handlers, reflow specs, ref captures — chained via Chain<T>(Action<T>?, Action<T>?), never clobbered), so a modifier can restyle everything but break nothing. Modifier rules: a PURE with-copy of its input; reading a signal inside one subscribes the OWNING CONTROL (modifiers run inside its Render — that's the granular :stuck restyle loop); never WRITE a signal in a modifier; no per-frame-hot reads (use FillBind/TransformBind for those); type-preserving (a modifier that changes the record type is ignored — parts style, content SLOTS like Content/HeaderContent restructure); avoid reshaping Children (structural reconcile).

List-UNIFORM (content-independent) item-chrome modifiers are cached apply-once via the parts epoch (TryApplyCached(string, Element, out Element)); per-item CONTENT differences use the PartDelta value seam (SelectorVisuals.cs), never a per-item modifier in a recycled scroll path.

TemplatePartsExtensions
TextEl
TextTiers

Color-tier modifiers for any TextEl: Ui.Body("x").Secondary().

Theme

Back-compat facade over the semantic token table (Tok). The flat names here mirror what existing call sites use; each forwards to the active TokenSet, so a theme swap (Use(ThemeKind)) re-themes them too. New code should prefer Tok / Type directly. The host still injects the OS accent + Mica background through the Accent/WindowBackground setters.

Tok

The active design-token table + the theme switch. Author UI reads tokens via the static getters (e.g. Tok.FillCardDefault); they resolve against T at zero per-read cost. Use(ThemeKind) re-themes the whole app with one pointer write. The OS accent + Mica window background are mutable overrides on top of the set, so they survive a theme swap and let the host inject the live system accent at startup.

TokenSet

An immutable, baked palette of every semantic Fluent brush for one theme (mirrors WinUI's *_themeresources). Built once per theme; never mutated. Read through Tok, which swaps the active set with a single pointer write.

Ui

Fluent C# markup. using static FluentGpu.Dsl.Ui; to author trees as expressions.

Enums

ThemeKind
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
